signatures.headline: Obsidian reported a plugin load or enable failure.
signatures.likelyCause: The deployed bundle, manifest, dependency graph, or plugin startup path failed before the plugin became usable.
nextActions: Confirm the deployed manifest id matches the target plugin id.
Check the first stack trace after reload and map it back to the built source.
Rebuild and redeploy only generated runtime artifacts before retrying reload.

signatures.headline: The plugin produced a JavaScript runtime exception.
signatures.likelyCause: Plugin startup, command handling, view rendering, or settings UI touched an undefined value or rejected asynchronously.
nextActions: Use the earliest stack trace after reload as the root symptom.
Add narrower timing or state logs around the failing startup/view path.
Fix the code path that throws before adding retries or longer waits.

matchAny: Cannot use import statement outside a module
signatures.headline: The deployed plugin bundle could not resolve a runtime module.
signatures.likelyCause: The build output, bundler format, external dependency configuration, or copied artifact set does not match what Obsidian can load.
nextActions: Inspect the generated main.js bundle and bundler external settings.
Verify deploy copied the current dist/main.js and manifest into the test vault.
Prefer bundling plugin runtime dependencies unless the dependency is intentionally provided by Obsidian.

signatures.headline: The plugin manifest or version metadata may be invalid for the target vault.
signatures.likelyCause: The manifest was missing, stale, malformed, or inconsistent with the deployed plugin id/version.
nextActions: Compare repo manifest.json, dist/manifest.json, and the test vault manifest.
Confirm id, version, main, and minAppVersion fields match the intended smoke target.
Rebuild before copying manifest files into the vault.

matchAny: The CLI is unable to find Obsidian
Please make sure Obsidian is running
signatures.headline: The Obsidian CLI could not reach a running desktop instance.
signatures.likelyCause: Obsidian is not running, the target vault is not focused, or the available obsidian executable is only the app launcher.
nextActions: Launch Obsidian desktop and open or focus the target test vault.
Retry obsidian help and confirm Developer commands are available.
Use the CDP-first path only after starting Obsidian with a reachable debug port.

signatures.headline: The CDP fallback could not attach to an Obsidian app target.
signatures.likelyCause: Obsidian was not launched with a debug port, the port is wrong, or automation attached to the wrong window.
nextActions: Probe http://127.0.0.1:9222/json/list before trusting CDP automation.
Restart Obsidian with the debug port helper when needed.
Use a vault title filter in multi-window setups.

signatures.headline: Plugin startup itself is slow before the view renders.
signatures.likelyCause: Heavy synchronous work still runs in the plugin onload/startup path.
nextActions: Split startup into labeled substeps.
Move non-essential work behind the initial render.
Compare cold-start and warm-start runs separately.

signatures.headline: View open or UI hydration is the visible bottleneck.
signatures.likelyCause: Tab restore, view construction, DOM rendering, or post-reload hydration is blocking visible UI readiness.
nextActions: Break the view-open path into restore, render, hydration, and post-render stages.
Render a usable shell before non-critical enrichment work.
Use a warm-start baseline to separate normal app variance from a regression.
